stacks.name: SG facilities-management market
stacks.note: US$3.65B (Mordor/6Wresearch, 2025) × ~1.30 SGD/USD. The often-cited 'S$3.65B' is the USD figure.

stacks.name: SG commercial security services (incremental)
stacks.note: ~47,000 officers × ~S$40k loaded ≈ S$1.9B contract value; ~30% sits outside the FM line.

stacks.name: SG remote video monitoring / VSaaS
stacks.note: APAC VSaaS US$1.66B (2025) × ~5% SG share × 1.30 FX.

stacks.name: Dormitory 'Manless Security' niche
stacks.note: ~1,000 addressable FEDA dorms × ≈ S$70k/dorm/yr monitoring + dispatch.

som.equation: S$9M internal + S$13.5M dormitory + S$5.5M IFM new logos

stacks.name: Internal Elitez / AB Associates sites (captive)
stacks.note: ~50 internal sites onboarded, no competitive bid.

stacks.name: Dormitory external clients
stacks.note: ~60 dorms won across the DTS retrofit window, ≈ S$70k/dorm/yr.

stacks.note: ~8 outcome-based FM contracts, ≈ S$350k avg ACV.

policies.title: Progressive Wage Model (security sector)
policies.body: Security-officer entry gross wage S$2,475 (Jan 2026) rising ~S$160/yr to S$2,795 by 2028; enforced as a PLRD licensing condition.
policies.implication: A legislated annual cost escalator on the labour alternative — command-centre ROI improves automatically; security-replacement demand is low-elasticity.

policies.title: Mandatory Outcome-Based Contracting (government security)
policies.body: All government agencies procure security on outcome basis since 1 May 2020; MHA/SPF push OBC into the private sector.
policies.implication: Procurement format that rewards a command-centre model over body-shop headcount bids.

policies.title: FEDA expansion + Dormitory Transition Scheme (DTS 2030)
policies.body: ~1,600 FEDA-licensed dorms (439,000 beds); ~1,000 must retrofit to DTS standards by 31 Dec 2030; DTS grant window 1 Mar–31 Aug 2026.
policies.implication: Time-boxed dormitory cross-sell — rank as attack-plan #1.

policies.title: PLRD security-agency licensing
policies.body: Agencies and officers licensed by SPF PLRD; ~240–255 licensed agencies.
policies.implication: Barrier to entry Elitez Security already clears; a pure-software/VSaaS competitor cannot legally deliver guarding-equivalent service.

policies.title: Smart Nation 2.0 / built-environment digitalisation
policies.body: IoT-sensor, cloud-dashboard, outcome-based service delivery pushed across the built environment.
policies.implication: Buyers already expect a digital service layer — Elitez sells into an expectation, not from zero.

implications.headline: Internal book de-risks the SOM — anchor pricing to it first
Dormitory 'Manless Security' is the sharpest external wedge — S$70M SAM, time-boxed
S$540M SAM means a margin/share play, not a land-grab
VSaaS line is small in SG — sell it as an embedded layer, not a standalone SKU

trends: Outcome-based contracting moving from government mandate to private-sector default
DTS 2030 retrofit wave creates a closing 2026–2030 dormitory cross-sell window
Security robotics + edge AI analytics crossing the cost threshold for routine commercial use
PWM wage escalator compounding through 2028 widens the labour-vs-monitored cost gap
